requirement.description: The BCM shall transmit whether the trunk is open or closed on the CAN bus.
requirement.python_test_setup:
import os
import time
import pytest
import can

BCM_CAN_ID = 0x100
TRUNK_STATUS_BIT = 4
DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5.0

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def can_bus():
    interface = os.getenv("CAN_INTERFACE", "socketcan")
    channel = os.getenv("CAN_CHANNEL", "can0")
    bitrate = int(os.getenv("CAN_BITRATE", "500000"))
    bus = can.Bus(interface=interface, channel=channel, bitrate=bitrate)
    yield bus
    bus.shutdown()

def get_trunk_status(data: bytes) -> int:
    return (data[0] >> TRUNK_STATUS_BIT) & 0x01

def collect_bcm_messages(can_bus, duration_sec=1.0):
    msgs = []
    end_time = time.time() + duration_sec
    while time.time() < end_time:
        msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=0.2)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == BCM_CAN_ID: msgs.append(msg)
    return msgs

def capture_transition(can_bus, initial, final, timeout=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT):
    seen_initial = False
    end_time = time.time() + timeout
    while time.time() < end_time:
        msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=0.5)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == BCM_CAN_ID:
            val = get_trunk_status(msg.data)
            if val == initial: seen_initial = True
            if seen_initial and val == final: return True
    return False

test_cases.title: Trunk Closed Verification
test_cases.python_test_script:
def test_tc_trk_001_trunk_closed(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_bcm_messages(can_bus, 2.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 5
    assert all(get_trunk_status(m.data) == 0 for m in msgs)

test_cases.title: Trunk Open Verification
test_cases.python_test_script:
def test_tc_trk_002_trunk_open(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_bcm_messages(can_bus, 2.0)
    assert len(msgs) > 5
    assert all(get_trunk_status(m.data) == 1 for m in msgs)

test_cases.title: Transition: Closed to Open
test_cases.expected_result: Trunk_Status changes from 0 to 1 within next frames

test_cases.title: Stability while Closed
Stability while Open
Robustness: Valid Values
Endurance: Open/Close Cycles
test_cases.expected_result: Each transition correctly reflected on CAN

test_cases.title: Interface: Correct CAN ID
test_cases.expected_result: Signal only present in CAN ID 0x100
test_cases.python_test_script:
def test_tc_trk_009_id_integrity(can_bus):
    # This assumes checking specifically for the signal behavior pattern
    msgs = collect_bcm_messages(can_bus, 5.0)
    assert all(m.arbitration_id == BCM_CAN_ID for m in msgs)

test_cases.title: Structural: Bit Position Integrity
Stress: Rapid Toggling
test_cases.python_test_script:
def test_tc_trk_011_rapid_toggle(can_bus):
    transitions = 0
    last_val = None
    end_time = time.time() + 10.0
    while time.time() < end_time:
        msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=0.1)
        if msg and msg.arbitration_id == BCM_CAN_ID:
            curr = get_trunk_status(msg.data)
            if last_val is not None and curr != last_val: transitions += 1
            last_val = curr
    assert transitions >= 5

test_cases.title: Persistence: Ignition Cycle
test_cases.python_test_script:
def test_tc_trk_012_persistence(can_bus):
    # Verification after manual ignition cycle
    msg = can_bus.recv(timeout=5.0)
    assert get_trunk_status(msg.data) == 1
